stocks.name: Wolfspeed Inc.
stocks.explanation: Wolfspeed surged on renewed momentum from its GE Aerospace partnership to co-develop high-voltage silicon carbide power modules for industrial, aerospace, and defense applications. The deal significantly expands Wolfspeed's addressable market beyond electric vehicles. Fifth-generation SiC MOSFET product launches and a broader semiconductor rally also contributed to gains.

stocks.name: Robinhood Markets
stocks.explanation: Robinhood jumped after Deutsche Bank analyst Brian Bedell raised his price target to $105 from $98, citing record month-to-date trading volumes across equities, options, and prediction markets. The platform is also benefiting from retail trading activity spurred by SpaceX's recent IPO, which raised $75 billion and became the largest IPO in Wall Street history.

stocks.name: ZoomInfo Technologies
stocks.explanation: ZoomInfo shares bounced back on a technical rebound after extended selling pressure following its recent earnings report where the company cut full-year 2026 revenue guidance and announced a 20% workforce reduction. The stock remains volatile as investors weigh the company's restructuring efforts against AI-driven headwinds in the sales intelligence market.

stocks.name: Applied Materials
stocks.explanation: Applied Materials rallied on multiple analyst upgrades tied to a strengthening semiconductor equipment capex cycle. Cantor Fitzgerald raised its price target to $650, citing a multi-year supply-constrained upcycle. The company's $500M Singapore campus expansion to support AI chip demand and bookings visibility stretching into 2028 underpinned bullish sentiment.

stocks.name: Butterfly Network
stocks.explanation: Butterfly Network shares gained as the medical imaging company continued to benefit from positive sentiment around AI-powered healthcare diagnostics. The company's handheld ultrasound devices, combined with AI image analysis, have attracted renewed investor interest amid broader AI adoption in clinical settings.

stocks.name: Western Digital
stocks.explanation: Western Digital climbed to new all-time highs on bullish analyst updates, including a Buy rating from J.P. Morgan, and strong Q3 2026 results showing revenue up 46% year-over-year to $3.34 billion. A 20% dividend increase to $0.15 per share, paid today, and surging data storage demand driven by AI infrastructure buildout fueled the rally.

stocks.name: Arm Holdings
stocks.explanation: Arm Holdings extended its year-to-date gain of over 270% as investors continue to price in the company's dominant position in AI and custom chip architectures. Bank of America recently raised its price target to $335 from $245. Management projections of $25 billion in annual revenue by 2031, driven significantly by AGI CPU sales, maintained bullish momentum.

stocks.name: ASML Holding
stocks.explanation: ASML advanced as the semiconductor equipment rally broadened. The company benefits from its monopoly position in extreme ultraviolet lithography, which is essential for producing the most advanced AI chips. Rising global fab investment and AI-driven chip demand continue to support the stock's premium valuation.

stocks.name: Vertiv Holdings
stocks.explanation: Vertiv Holdings gained alongside the broader AI infrastructure trade. The company's power management and thermal solutions for data centers remain in high demand as hyperscalers accelerate AI buildouts. Analysts view Vertiv as a key beneficiary of the multi-year data center expansion cycle.

stocks.name: Sea Limited
stocks.explanation: Sea Limited rose as the Southeast Asian tech conglomerate continues its profitability momentum. Investors are optimistic about the company's ability to leverage AI across its Shopee e-commerce platform and digital financial services while maintaining disciplined cost management.

stocks.name: Synopsys Inc.
stocks.explanation: Synopsys gained on the semiconductor equipment rally and continued demand for its electronic design automation tools, which are critical for developing AI-optimized chip architectures. The company's AI-driven design tools are seeing accelerating adoption among chipmakers racing to develop next-generation processors.

stocks.name: Broadcom Inc.
stocks.explanation: Broadcom advanced ahead of its closely watched earnings report this week. The company is expected to show strong growth in its AI-related custom chip and networking businesses. Analysts are focused on VMware integration progress and the trajectory of AI accelerator revenue.

stocks.name: Marvell Technology
stocks.explanation: Marvell Technology rose on continued optimism around its custom AI chip and data center networking businesses. The company has secured key design wins with major hyperscalers and is positioned to benefit from expanding AI infrastructure spending.

stocks.name: Amkor Technology
stocks.explanation: Amkor Technology advanced with the semiconductor rally as demand for advanced packaging used in AI chips continues to grow. The company's presence in high-bandwidth memory packaging and system-in-package technology positions it well for the AI buildout cycle.

stocks.name: Seagate Technology
stocks.explanation: Seagate Technology surged to new all-time highs alongside Western Digital on bullish analyst updates and record data storage demand driven by AI workloads. The company's enterprise hard drive business is benefiting from hyperscaler data center expansions and growing AI training dataset requirements.
